author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
Pelagius Professes Nothing on the Subject of Grace Which May Not Be Understood of the Law and Teaching.
title: Pelagius Professes Nothing on the Subject of Grace Which May Not Be Understood of the Law and Teaching.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Chapter 33 [XXXI.]—Pelagius Professes Nothing on the Subject of Grace Which May Not Be Understood of the Law and Teaching.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: “See,” he says, “how this epistle will clear me before your Blessedness; for in it we clearly and simply declare, that we possess a free will which is unimpaired for sinning and for not sinning; and this free will is in all good works always assisted by divine help.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Now you perceive, by the understanding which the Lord has given you, that these words of his are inadequate to solve the question.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: For it is still open to us to inquire what the help is by which he would say that the free will is assisted; lest perchance he should, as is usual with him, maintain that law and teaching are meant.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: If, indeed, you were to ask him why he used the word “always,” he might answer: Because it is written, And in His law will he meditate day and night.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Then, after interposing a statement about the condition of man, and his natural capacity for sinning and not sinning, he added the following words: “Now this power of free will we declare to reside generally in all alike—in Christians, in Jews, and in Gentiles.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: In all men free will exists equally by nature, but in Christians alone is it assisted by grace.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: And again he might answer: “By the law and the Christian teaching.”
